summary: Verified 3 new changes on D2Synergy vanilla-JS app: (1) Per-panel Clear buttons + sticky top header, (2) Daily & Weekly Activities overlay wired to public Bungie Milestones, (3) Vendor Inventories overlay. All 8 acceptance criteria PASS. Zero uncaught console errors or page exceptions. Bungie public /Destiny2/Milestones/ call is being reached (no CORS block) but Bungie itself returned HTTP 500 five times — the app then displayed its exact graceful fallback error, which the review request explicitly accepts.

integration_issues.issue: Bungie's public /Destiny2/Milestones/ endpoint returned HTTP 500 on all 5 retries during this test run. This is a Bungie-side outage/rate condition, NOT a CORS block (a CORS block would surface as a browser NetworkError with no HTTP status; here we got real 500 responses). The app handled it gracefully with the visible message 'Could not load activities: Bungie API HTTP 500 — the request itself failed (network issue, CORS, or Bungie.net outage)'. Retest later when Bungie is healthy.

issues: When /Destiny2/Milestones/ fails, the body's own .resetChip 'Daily reset:'/'Weekly reset (Tue):' cards are NOT rendered (chips are appended AFTER the try/catch success branch in loadActivities, line 4698-4701). The header sub-title '#activitiesReset' still shows 'Daily reset in Xh Ym • Weekly reset (Tue) in Xh Ym', so the countdowns aren't completely lost — but the review-mentioned body chips only appear on the success path. Cosmetic: move the resetChips block above the bungieGet call so users still see the countdowns even when Bungie is down.

test_1_page_loads_and_sticky_header.details: Title='D2Synergy'. header.top position=sticky (top:0, z-index:60). After scrolling to y=3000, header stays with getBoundingClientRect().top === 0. Reset Build button remains reachable at all scroll positions.

test_2_all_7_clear_buttons_present.details: [data-testid='clear-panel-p1'..'clear-panel-p7'] all exist, each showing text 'Clear'. Panel labels covered: p1 Class & Subclass, p2 Exotic Armor, p3 Exotic Weapon, p4 Tiered Legendary Weapons, p5 Armor Mods, p6 Seasonal Artifact, p7 Legendary Armor Set Bonus.

test_3_clear_scope_isolation.details: Setup: In p1 selected aspect 'Sol Invictus' (chip.active), fragment 'Ember of Ashes' (chip.active), grenade 'Solar Grenade' (select value). In p5 Helmet Slot 1 selected 'Harmonic Siphon'. Action: clicked [data-testid='clear-panel-p1']. Result: p1 activeChips reduced from ['Hammer of Sol','Sol Invictus','Ember of Ashes'] to just ['Hammer of Sol'] (default super restored by design, per clearPanel case 'p1'); grenade select value became empty; p5 Helmet Slot 1 select value REMAINED 'Harmonic Siphon' unchanged. p1 panel body 'collapsed' class was false both before and after the Clear click (stopPropagation prevented header toggle). No other panels changed.

test_4_drawer_menu_new_rows.details: Hamburger drawer contains all 3 rows: [data-testid='open-fullscreen-vault-row'] text='My Vault ❯', [data-testid='open-vendors-row'] text='Vendor Inventories ❯', [data-testid='open-activities-row'] text='Daily & Weekly Activities ❯'.

test_5_activities_overlay_open_and_count.status: PASS (with graceful error, per review criteria)
test_5_activities_overlay_open_and_count.details: Clicking open-activities-row adds 'open' class to [data-testid='activities-overlay']. Header sub-title immediately populated with reset countdowns: 'Daily reset in 12h 27m • Weekly reset (Tue) in 12h 27m'. Public call to https://www.bungie.net/Platform/Destiny2/Milestones/ returned HTTP 500 (5 retries all 500). App then rendered exact graceful fallback in body: 'Could not load activities: Bungie API HTTP 500 — the request itself failed (network issue, CORS, or Bungie.net outage)'. Zero uncaught console errors, zero page exceptions. IMPORTANT for main agent: this is an outage-path outcome, NOT a CORS block.

test_6_activities_close_via_button_and_e.details: Clicking [data-testid='activities-close-btn'] removes 'open' class from [data-testid='activities-overlay']. Re-opening then pressing Escape also removes 'open' class.

test_7_vendors_overlay_not_signed_in.details: Clicking [data-testid='open-vendors-row'] opens [data-testid='vendors-overlay'] (adds 'open'). Body text exactly reads 'Could not load vendors: Not signed in. Open the menu → Sign in with Bungie.net first. Vendor data needs the ReadDestinyVendorsAndAdvisors permission (you may be asked to re-authorize once). Sign-in only works on the deployed GitHub Pages site.' No crash/hang. [data-testid='vendors-search-input'], [data-testid='vendors-reload-btn'], [data-testid='vendors-close-btn'] all present.

test_8_no_uncaught_console_errors.details: Page error listener captured 0 uncaught exceptions across the full flow (page load, drawer, aspect/fragment click, grenade select, helmet mod select, clear-panel-p1 click, activities open+wait+close, vendors open+close). Console error listener captured only network-level 'Failed to load resource: 500' entries for the Bungie Milestones endpoint — these are logged by the browser but handled by the app's try/catch (no thrown exception).

cors_verdict: Bungie DOES allow cross-origin calls from https://synergy-builder-1.preview.emergentagent.com — the fetch returned real HTTP 500 responses (not CORS-blocked). If it were CORS, browser would surface 'blocked by CORS policy' and the fetch would reject with NetworkError, not HTTP 500. Main agent: safe to keep direct api.bungie.net client-side calls; the Preview URL is not being blocked.
exact_activities_error_text: Could not load activities: Bungie API HTTP 500 — the request itself failed (network issue, CORS, or Bungie.net outage)
exact_vendors_error_text: Could not load vendors: Not signed in. Open the menu → Sign in with Bungie.net first. Vendor data needs the ReadDestinyVendorsAndAdvisors permission (you may be asked to re-authorize once). Sign-in only works on the deployed GitHub Pages site.
action_items: (Optional cosmetic only) Move the resetChips body-render block above the bungieGet('/Destiny2/Milestones/') call inside loadActivities() (index.html ~line 4698) so 'Daily reset:' / 'Weekly reset (Tue):' chips stay visible in the body even when Bungie returns an error. Currently the countdowns only survive in the smaller header sub-title on failure.

(Optional) Wait a while and re-check the activities window to confirm the happy-path activity-card rendering; today the public /Destiny2/Milestones/ endpoint was intermittently 500ing from Bungie's side, so live-card verification could not be completed.
critical_code_review_comments: index.html is now 4876 lines — far past the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting builder rendering, overlays, Bungie OAuth/sync, and manifest cache into ES modules. Not a bug — informational (repeat comment from iteration_3).

In loadActivities(): the resetChips element is only appended in the try{} success branch (line 4698). If bungieGet('/Destiny2/Milestones/') throws, users lose the body chips entirely. Consider appending the chips before the try{} so the countdowns render regardless of API state.

In getManifestEntity() the try/catch inside the async IIFE lacks a catch(err){ delete __entInFlight[key]; throw err; } path — currently a rejection propagates but the finally block already deletes the in-flight entry so this is actually fine; noting for completeness.

clearPanel('p1') resets state.super to defaultSuperFor(cls, element) rather than null. This is intentional (the super grid always shows one active chip), but the user-observed effect is that a chip remains .active after Clear. Consider adding a tiny visual hint ('Super defaulted') so users don't think Clear didn't work fully.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 8 acceptance criteria pass, with activities error-path being the review-accepted alternative outcome)

test_credentials: N/A — sign-in explicitly out of scope; 'Not signed in' path verified for vendors.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vanilla-JS static app at /app/frontend/public/index.html. `state` object is module-scoped (const state = {...}) — NOT exposed on window; use DOM inspection ('.chip.active', select.value) instead of window.state. p5 Armor Mods panel body: p5Body.children[0..4] map to Helmet, Arms, Chest, Legs, ClassItem wraps (each is a plain <div> with inline borderBottom style). Each wrap contains 6 .slotrow rows (Tier, Slot1, Slot2, Slot3, Slot4, Masterwork) + a Tuning select (Tier 5 only) + energy readout. Match Slot 1..4 by .slotrow > label textContent = 'Slot 1' etc. p1 aspects/fragments are .chip elements; selected ones get the .active class. Default super for the class/element combo (e.g. Titan Solar => 'Hammer of Sol') is ALWAYS a .chip.active by design — filter it out when asserting 'aspects cleared'. Bungie public /Destiny2/Milestones/ was 500-ing during this run so activities loaded via the error path; the app handles the failure with a specific in-body error message and does not throw.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs to root-cause. The one behavioral observation — activities body missing the .resetChip countdown cards when Bungie 500s — is a minor cosmetic UX gap, not a bug. Root cause: chips are appended inside the try{} success branch (line 4698). Mitigation: hoist the resetChips element append above the try{} block so the countdowns survive an API failure.
